calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration

Petition To Direct Administrator To Sell Real Property
Off Calendar Mar-16-2026 Text Ruling

Petition To Select And Set Apart Probate Homestead [Probate Code Section 6520]
Off Calendar Mar-20-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Wrobel and Jeff Coons for administrator Consuelo Rios, who was also present (via Zoom).
Chris Land for Margarita Martinez, Attorney-in-Fact for Charis Kihewa, successor-in-interest to Eloy Martinez (via Zoom).
Elizabeth Martinez, daughter of Consuelo Rios (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on: 1) Petition to Select and Set Apart Probate Homestead; and 2) Petition to Direct Administrator to Sell Real Property.

Mr. Land reported that the parties had reached a settlement and stated that his client would withdraw her objections to the homestead petition and dismiss the petition to direct sale of the property. Mr. Coons and Mr. Wrobel stated they were not aware of any further objections and requested that the Court grant the homestead petition.

Based on the parties' representations, the Court dismissed the Petition to Direct Administrator to Sell Real Property and took the Petition to Select and Set Apart Probate Homestead under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Off Calendar Mar-09-2026 Granted Per Written Order

Petition To Select And Set Apart Probate Homestead [Probate Code Section 6520]
Off Calendar Feb-02-2026 Continued To Mar-16-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing. The Personal Representative Shall File Either A Petition For Order For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report Of Status Of Administration Pursuant To Prob. Code Sec.12200 Et Seq. Two Weeks Before The Hearing Date.
Off Calendar Nov-19-2025 Continued To Feb-02-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Select And Set Apart Probate Homestead [Probate Code Section 6520]
Off Calendar Nov-10-2025 Continued To Feb-02-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Wrobel for administrator Consuelo Rios, who was also present (via Zoom).
Christopher Land and John Kithas for Margarita Martinez, Attorney-in-Fact for Charis Kihewa, successor-in-interest to Eloy Martinez (via Zoom).
Elizabeth Martinez, daughter of Consuelo Rios, was also present (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Petition to Select and Set Apart Probate Homestead.

Mr. Wrobel argued that Ms. Rios, age 79, had lived in the home since the 1970s, was the decedent's long-term partner and later spouse, contributed to the household, and has received assignments from some heirs. He acknowledged that separate counsel for Ms. Rios on the homestead issue might be appropriate.

Mr. Land argued that the estate has been pending for four years, that Ms. Rios had previously been ordered to file a petition for final distribution or status report and did not do so, and that seeking a homestead now was adverse to the heirs and inconsistent with the Court's orders, especially where a petition to sell the property is already set for hearing on February 2, 2026.

Elizabeth Martinez spoke about her mother's age, long residence, fixed income, reliance on public transit, and strong community ties.

After discussion, the Court continued the matter to February 2, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to be heard with the petition to sell real property. The parties were invited to confer and supplement the record before that date.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing. The Personal Representative Shall File Either A Petition For Order For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report Of Status Of Administration Pursuant To Prob. Code Sec.12200 Et Seq. Two Weeks Before The Hearing Date.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-26-2023 Continued To Sep-18-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Consuelo Rios, Administrator, and daughter Elizabeth Martinez, via Zoom, John Kithas, Esq., via Zoom, for Eloy Martinez, Interested Party.

The court recognizes Mr. Meredith's current health status and confirms with the Administrator and her daughter that they are aware of the same. Ms. Martinez reports that her mother has potentially found new counsel who intends to work with Mr. Meredith to take over this matter. Mr. Kithas suggests the appointment of third-party Leo Bautista as successor Administrator. The court advises Ms. Martinez as to what the appointment of Mr. Bautista would mean in this matter. The court advises that it does not feel comfortable with further discussions without Administrator's counsel present.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Sep-18-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-27-2023 Continued To Jun-26-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Kithas appeared by zoom for Eloy Martinez (beneficiary).
Roger Meredith appeared by zoom for and with Consuelo Rios (administrator) by zoom.
Johanna Valle (interpreter) in person.

The interpreter stated for the record: Her name is Johanna Valle. Her Certification Number is 376919. She has an oath on file with the Court. The language to be interpreted is Spanish to English and English to Spanish.

The court informed Mr. Meredith that it is looking for a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of a report on status of administration, and that a declaration is not what it's looking for. Mr. Meredith stated that he read the examiner's notes and understands, but the declaration is how the estate is right now. Mr. Meredith also stated that there is a title issue with a Los Angeles property, that the LA property was not brought to his attention until now, and that the real estate broker in LA indicated that the property in question APN is also held by a second property. In addition, Mr. Meredith stated that the other parcel is not owned by the decedent at this moment and that the broker is dealing with the title company to resolve this issue. The Court noted that Mr. Meredith's focus on the LA property leaves out the San Francisco property. Mr. Meredith spoke of the administrator and her two children's intention to buy out the interest of the other beneficiaries, stated that several of the other beneficiaries who are not the children of Mrs. Rios have agreed to gift their interests to Mrs. Rios, and that his client's daughter and son have the ability to make the pay out to the other beneficiaries. The court stated that the path forward is to get this reduced to writing and filed in a formal manner which will permit Mr. Kithas to make appropriate written objections. Mr. Kithas stated that he doesn't really have any objection to the concept of having the other beneficiaries bought out, but there are several other issues that need to be resolved before moving forward, and that he is not aware of any appraisal on the San Francisco property. Mr. Kithas also raised the issue of not being certain about what the financing terms will be for the son and the daughter, or how much the daughter has contributed to the estate, raised the question about whether or not Mrs. Rios should be charged rental value for two years of living in the property without paying rent, and offered to work with Mr. Meredith to smooth things out. Mr. Meredith stated that the property was appraised by a probate referee and that it was sent to Mr. Kithas. Mr. Kithas noted that it is a date of death value. Status on close of estate is continued to June 26, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Meredith is to file either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of a report on status of administration.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar May-18-2022 Continued To Aug-01-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Apr-06-2022 Continued To May-18-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Roger Meredith, via Zoom, for Consuelo Rios, via Zoom, Petitioner.

The court points to the previous calling of this matter where there were no appearances by counsel or party. Counsel offers testimony regarding why he failed to appear and accepts full responsibility for the oversight. Counsel reports that his client was not bondable until recently. Counsel reports that there are two real properties that are currently being appraised and once those appraisals are completed, he will file the Inventory and Appraisal.

After additional discussion, including brief testimony by Ms. Rios via the assistance of the Interpreter shown below, the Status of Filing of Inventory and Appraisal is continued to May-18-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Counsel is advised that if the Inventory and Appraisal is not ready to be filed by May-11-2022, he is instructed to file a brief status report as to why, by the same date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Gomez. Not reported. Spanish Interpreter: Shannon Raintree/Certification #: 300563.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Apr-06-2022 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Mar-09-2022 Continued To Apr-06-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person. An Order to Appear will be issued to Personal Representative Consuelo Rios and Attorney Roger Meredith to appear on Apr-06-2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why the inventory and appraisal has not been filed. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Andam. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Inventory And Appraisal

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-01-2021 Denied

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-22-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Roger Meredith, via ZOOM, for Consuelo Rios, Objector; John Kithas, via ZOOM, for Eloy Martinez, Petitioner; Marta Eisenberg, via ZOOM, for Leo Bautista, via ZOOM, proposed Personal Representative.

Mr. Meredith reports that one of the children of the decedent has passed away in Mexico. The court queries counsel if they might be interested in mediation. Mr. Meredith believes that mediation would not be productive without additional information. Mr. Kithas believes that mediation would help this matter reach a resolution. The court advises the parties that it does not believe that the appointment of a professional fiduciary makes sense at this time. The court denies Eloy Martinez' petition in open court and states that it intends to appoint Consuelo Rios as Administrator. The court states that it will be setting the following status dates: Status on Filing of Inventory and Appraisal on Mar-01-2022 and Status on Close of Estate on Nov-15-2022 - If a Petition is filed by Nov-01-2022, an appearance will not be required on Nov-15-2022. The court advises the parties to file a drop-off ex parte petition if they wish to mediate.

The Petition for Letters of Administration is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
